programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Niklas Ekstedt, and special guest Dev Griffin. Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads to the hustle and bustle of Marrakesh to explore every side of Moroccan cuisine. She rustles up some quick dishes inspired by the aromatic flavours and smells of the medina quarter, from the mounds of olives and heady spices at the markets to the slow-cooked tagines prepared in the heart of a family kitchen and the high-end delights of Moha Fedal. one of the country's greatest chefs. Mary brings the fragrant flavours home, cooking a speedy lamb tagine, a spicy carrot hummus and a fresh summer salad(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Norwich City v Manchester City and Liverpool v Newcastle United in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents as the hunt for bargains heads to Oswestry, Shropshire, where experts Jonathan Pratt and John Cameron assist two teams of friends competing to buy three items in the hope of making the most profit at auction. Natasha also heads to Hawarden, Flintstone, to visit Gladstone's Library, Britain's only prime ministerial library, where she reveals what a wheelbarrow and a former prime minister have in common(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired detective and his wife to look for a home in Gloucestershire on a £500,000 budget, and goes behind the scenes ahead of the Badminton Horse Trials(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items bound for the skip in Walsall, featuring a Lloyd Loom-style laundry basket, a reclining chair and an oak writing bureau(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A special programme focusing on Strictly Come Dancing's professional dancers, looking back at their journeys to reach the ballroom and some of their best moments from the show. The programme also offers viewers a behind-the-scenes look at life on Strictly, revealing some of the show's most guarded secrets(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A Radio 2 edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Jo Whiley, Gary Davies, Ken Bruce, Angela Scanlon, Craig Charles, Trevor Nelson, the Rev Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad, and a special performance from Rick Astley. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: The classical music celebration continues live from the Royal Albert Hall. Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Chorus and the BBC Singers through an energetic programme, including the Overture to Offenbach's Orpheus in the Underworld, Grainger's Marching Song of Democracy, Harold Arlen's Somewhere Over the Rainbow and Gershwin's I Got Rhythm. The event is then brought to its familiar and rousing close with Rule, Britannia!, Land of Hope and Glory and Jerusalem. Presented by Katie Derham(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v Manchester City at Carrow Road and Liverpool v Newcastle United at Anfield. The Canaries had not beaten their opponents at home since way back in August 2001 in the old First Division, although the two most recent Premier League encounters between the sides on this ground finished in very creditable 0-0 draws. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Burnley at the Amex Stadium, Manchester United v Leicester City at Old Trafford, Sheffield United v Southampton at Bramall Lane, Tottenham Hotspur v Crystal Palace at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Chelsea at Molineux(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan give the young artists the opportunity of a lifetime, as they work with Jae 5 and perform live for Lady Leshurr(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Carolina Panthers v Tampa Bay Buccaneers at Bank of America Stadium(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v Manchester City at Carrow Road and Liverpool v Newcastle United at Anfield. The Canaries had not beaten their opponents at home since way back in August 2001 in the old First Division, although the two most recent Premier League encounters between the sides on this ground finished in very creditable 0-0 draws. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Burnley at the Amex Stadium, Manchester United v Leicester City at Old Trafford, Sheffield United v Southampton at Bramall Lane, Tottenham Hotspur v Crystal Palace at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Chelsea at Molineux(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics North West
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the sale and development of a third-floor flat in Southwark, London, a piece of land in Derbyshire's Chesterfield, and flats in Hengoed, Mid Glamorgan(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits the sacred Christian site of Lourdes in France, and meets a British man who believes he was miraculously cured there. Plus, a performance of Ave Maria(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a green-fingered couple in Gloucestershire find their perfect retreat with plenty of space to enjoy their hobbies, on a budget of £800,000(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich Brothers compete to design a £5,000 family garden in Southampton, ensuring it is safe for toddler Alex(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items salvaged from the country's tips and turns them into new pieces to make a profit, aided by three of the nation's master craftsmen(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series' biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOS's most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kerry Howard, Neil Stuke, Robert Duncan, Susannah Doyle, Brigit Forsyth, James Baxter, Simon Greenall and Pauline McLynn(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets a larger-than-life character in Dorset - The Cerne Abbas Giant. It's 100 years since the Giant was gifted to the National Trust, and he needs to be smartened up, so Ellie joins a group of volunteers to re-chalk the figure, hoping to restore it to its former glory. Steve Brown visits the world's only managed flock of Mute swans at Abbotsbury Swannery, while Adam Henson meets the Welsh and Scottish contenders hoping to lift this year's One Man and His Dog trophy(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where surprises include a mysterious object linked to the Titanic, a well-used watch with connections to early moon exploration and an undiscovered letter written by Winston Churchill. There are also items of museum quality, such as an exquisite piece of Indian textile and a prized collection of Gurkha medals(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of the party, Tommy reveals his intentions for Oswald Mosley, and someone close to the family finds themselves targeted. Arthur and his men arrive at Limehouse for the arranged rendezvous, but the situation has changed drastically, while Tommy visits an old war comrade who has a very specific skill that Tommy intends to put to good use. Starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Bournemouth v Everton at the Vitality Stadium and Watford v Arsenal at Vicarage Road. Bournemouth and the Toffees drew last season's corresponding fixture 2-2 after a thrilling second half that saw a red card for each team and four goals. Arsenal were looking for a fourth successive victory over the Hornets, though Watford were hindered by Troy Deeney's early sending off when they lost 1-0 at home to the Gunners last term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Lancashire Police's new academy, where the forensic scientists of tomorrow will train alongside the police teams solving real crimes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers pursue those responsible for an horrific crash that scarred an entire community, and raid the luxury home of an illegal money lender preying on the vulnerable(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughterare lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Phil and Theresa cling on to their last bit of hope as the court decides on baby Josh's fate. Al finds an unexpected confidante and Valerie obsesses about Daniel and Becky(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of the south east of England. Along the way, they encounter items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: Ben Moore meets the villagers who feel under attack by noisy motorbikes day and night - and the bikers who say they're proud to be loud as it makes them safer on our roads. As a national shortage of professional chefs threatens our curry houses, food entrepreneur Sarah Ali Choudhury, who grew up in her family's restaurant in Dorset, is calling for more women to get involved(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the most important day of the year for Kerry - the annual steam fair. This year it falls on her birthday and Kurtan has organised her a surprise present. However, the gift involves some walking, and so the cousins embark on a cross-country trek. This affords them the time to share some local village myths with the crew, such as the legend of the 'Fox Twins'(n)

programme.title: Murder in Successville
programme.desc: Sleet and sidekick Emma Bunton go to North Successville, where the detective investigates the murder of a fellow cop and takes a liking to the area's chief of police. Three likely suspects soon emerge - the owner of a diner, a local fisherman and a disgraced former police officer. An approaching storm forces Sleet and Emma to seek shelter in a forest - which leads to a showdown with the killer(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, treating the audience to her own brand of humour, before introducing routines by camp comic Joe Lycett and award-winning funnyman Russell Kane(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: Ali-A helps young surfer Sonny transform his family's old ex-post van for ever, both inside and out(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Bella ends up in a spiral of self doubt after missing a goal(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The studio is transformed into a Batcave to celebrate Batman's 80th anniversary(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Steve Leonard and Miranda Krestovnikoff for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: The Animal's Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham focuses on coastal creatures and investigates why the UK's waters are the most popular place in the world for the grey seal and Manx shearwater. He also champions the misunderstood British gull and finds out how the shore crab and bottlenose dolphin have extraordinary ways of sensing their environment(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Six more teams compete in the fourth heat, as robots Iron-Awe 6, the Kegs, Tauron, Nuts 2, Concussion and Androne 4000 battle through a series of group battles and knock-out rounds. Among the contestants controlling the machines is a veteran of the series, returning to defend his legacy. Presented by Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties in Westgate on Sea, Kent, Cleator Moor in Cumbria, and a house in Guildford which may need demolishing(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel's final leg of his tour of modern British home cooking explores the flair and flamboyance of noodles, including his own rustic take on old-fashioned spaghetti bolognese. Steffi introduces him to the German art of noodle-making from scratch using a traditional spatzle board, and he is also shown Spanish and Thai recipes as he meets two other home cooks(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner discusses culinary memories with Anneka Rice, revisiting her childhood in Surrey, and creates two dishes that take her back to that time(n)

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: Edinburgh chef Tony Singh travels to the state of Punjab to trace his family's roots and discover whether his mum's Punjabi cooking is authentic. He visits Amritsar during the festival of Diwali, receives an exclusive invitation to eat and cook with a maharajah and meets the Nihang, an order of nomadic Sikh warriors. Tony discovers how the Sikh religion and the Punjab's troubled past have created a food and culture like no other, and ends his epic adventure at his ancestral home in New Delhi with his 93-year-old uncle(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Impressionist Jon Culshaw retraces the Edinburgh and Scottish Highlands coach trip holiday he enjoyed with his family when he was 12 years old. Len surprises him by arranging a 1980s vehicle for the tour, and along the way they take in some of the places Jon would have visited back in the day(n)

programme.title: Jungle Atlantis
programme.desc: Part two of two. The experts continue their attempt to discover why Angkor suddenly collapsed, using a revolutionary technology called lidar to reveal the true scale and splendour of this abandoned megacity. Deep in the Cambodian jungle, they have discovered a sophisticated network of roads and canals that are the forgotten world of Angkor's greatest king, shedding new light on the dramatic events leading to the fall of the city(n)

programme.title: Nature's Boldest Thieves
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke investigates the reasons why urban-dwelling seagulls, foxes, badgers and squirrels have become so successful at stealing people's food. The presenter visits St Ives in Cornwall, where seagulls swoop daily to steal sandwiches out of people's hands, and receives help from avian experts Graham Martin and Steven Portugal to discover what makes the birds such good thieves. The host learns that up to 60 per cent of an urban fox's diet now comprises human food, and observes a black squirrel tackling a home-made assault course in a Hertfordshire garden in a bid to steal nuts from a bird feeder(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Scotland, volunteers from Dunbar race to save three children trapped in a cave by rising tides, while in the west, the Tobermory crew comes to the aid of a yacht caught in a storm and being forced onto the rocks. In England, the Blyth crew searches for an 80-year-old man who has gone missing after leaving home to look for lobsters on the coast(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Under Captain Square's influence, Mainwaring has too much to drink and suffers a terrible hangover during a weekend course - but still manages to save Jonesy's life. Comedy, first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Katie Derham hosts the climax of the classical music festival live from the Royal Albert Hall. American mezzo-soprano Jamie Barton joins Sakari Oramo and the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Chorus and the BBC Singers in a programme that includes a world premiere by Daniel Kidane, ballet music from Manuel de Falla's The Three-Cornered Hat, Laura Mvula performing Sing to the Moon, plus classics by Bizet, Saint-Saëns and Verdi(n)

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Western, starring Armie Hammer, Johnny Depp and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: One half of The Mighty Boosh and co-host of The Great British Bake Off Noel Fielding takes over the hosting reins of the long-running show as he comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The comedian, actor, artist, DJ and musician treats the audience to his own peculiar brand of surreal humour, before introducing routines by two up-and-coming stars of the comedy circuit, with south Londoner Dane Baptiste and Irishman Al Porter taking to the stage at the famous venue(n)

programme.title: Golf: Solheim Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of day two of the women's team competition between Europe and USA, held this year at Gleneagles in Perthshire. During the last contest in 2017 at Des Moines Golf and Country Club, Europe began day two trailing by three points and got off to a nightmare start by losing the opening two foursomes matches. They rallied to level the session before the USA triumphed in three of the afternoon's four-ball ties to take a commanding overall five-point lead into the third day(n)

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: The horticulturist presents a year-long diary about the life cycle of her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, where she offers seasonal advice and guidance, beginning with how to look after plants throughout January and February. She prunes the greenery, prepares the soil and plants garlic, before a local woodsman helps her lay a native hedge(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter W, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: An extended edition sees Monty Don add autumn perennials to his borders, show how to take root cuttings and reveal plans for next year when he plants bulbs for spring colour. On her Kent allotment, Frances Tophill catches up on some seasonal sowing and invites a special guest to help her with a small building project. Nick Bailey visits Cambridge University Botanic Garden, where he finds out how and why some plants produce their scent at the end of the day. The programme also returns to the wildlife garden of Kate Bradbury to find out about the lives of solitary bees(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a 2,000-mile journey along the famous American road, taking them through eight different states, each with their own history, flavours and people. They begin by learning about Chicago's meatpacking industry, before moving on to small-town American, where they spend time with the Amish community, cooking together and learning about their different way of life. Their first leg ends in St Louis, where they try 'concrete custard' - a treat so rich and thick it has to be served upside down(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The seven heat winners are up against each other in the grand final, as they compete for the title of best up and coming artisan. First, they are tasked to supply a shop for a month, but they will not be there themselves to tempt customers to buy. Then, they return to the shop for one last farmers' market for the locals, before judges scrutinise the finalists' marketing and products one final time(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of three terraced properties in Cardiff open their doors to five neighbours in the hope of being judged as having the outstanding house in their locality(n)

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin presents the first of two programmes exploring animal relationships, finding out how they meet, mate and form relationships, and asking whether they can fall in love the same as humans. On her travels, she finds several animals that have discovered the secrets of successful long-lasting relationship, including the bonobo of the Congo, Africa's wild dogs and tamarins in the UK. But there are other sides to the story, like the devoted penguin couple that stayed together for 10 years, only for things to turn out not quite as they seemed. Liz also hears how a Siamaon gibbon dealt with the death of his life partner and meets a pair of elephants with an extraordinary friendship(n)

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary exploring the country in the aftermath of the recent humanitarian crisis in which thousands of Rohingya Muslims were driven from their homes by the military. Simon visits Burma's Buddhist heartlands, including the ancient capital of Bagan, and meets the city's controversial nationalist monks. While the region affected by the military crackdown is off limits, he is able to visit the world's biggest refugee camp in Bangladesh, and meet people traumatised by the violence(n)

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon ventures into Burma's highlands, where he attends a fire balloon festival and visits an elephant sanctuary, where the animals can enjoy a peaceful life after working in the logging industry. He also makes a secret journey into one of the country's conflict zones to meet members of a rebel army fighting against the Burmese military(n)

programme.title: Golf: Solheim Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the third and final day of the women's team competition between Europe and USA, held this year at Gleneagles in Perthshire, where 12 singles matches were played. The Europeans performed credibly in this session last year in Iowa, tying it 6-6, but it ensured they fell to an overall 16.5-11.5 defeat after the Americans had dominated the opening two days(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Panellists Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Theo Paphitis assess the profit-making potential of an inventive new solution to clearing up post-festival rubbish, a means of balancing gut bacteria from a sauerkraut enthusiast, and a new kind of phone alarm app. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: A report on schoolgirls used as suicide bombers in Nigeria by terrorist group Boko Haram. In the city of Maidugur, Stacey talks to Falmata, who was kidnapped by Boko Haram at the age of 13, forced to marry three times, and finally strapped to a suicide belt and sent out on a bombing mission. She managed to escape to tell her painful story. But not all women in Boko have been forced to join. Ammabua believed in the group's ideology and volunteered for a suicide-bombing mission, which she thought would send her to paradise. Fate intervened and she survived. Now separated from Boko Haram, she is trying to re-integrate into a society of people she was once willing to kill(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom has an awkward encounter, which affects his conversation with Louise about fading passion. Comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom tries talking Louise into helping him with a silly lie, but presenting a united front proves tricky. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: The Company You Keep (2012)
programme.desc: A respected lawyer is forced to go on the run when a reporter exposes the truth about him - he is really a member of a radical militant group wanted for questioning in a murder case, who has been living under a fake identity for 30 years. As the fugitive goes in search of his old allies for sanctuary, the reporter delves into his past and uncovers disturbing secrets. Thriller based on the novel by Neil Gordon, directed by and starring Robert Redford, with Shia LaBeouf, Julie Christie and Susan Sarandon(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second edition of the topical debate - originally billed as the first of a new series until another episode was sneaked in last week. As usual she will be inviting the studio audience - this time a venue in London - to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A fishing boat in Salcombe goes up in flames with the skipper still aboard, while on the Isle of Sheppey, the lifeboat crew race to the aid of a man stuck in freezing mud in the middle of the night. A dog falls into the river Thames, creating an emergency for the Chiswick crew when the owner dives in to save it - and soon needs rescuing herself(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House-proud couples from the seaside towns of Weston-super-Mare and Bournemouth swap homes to try out a new way of life, and see whether they enjoy the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A search for relatives helps to bring back together a family who have spent a lifetime apart. Plus, the story of a man who helped changed public perception of disability guide dogs(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. Fiinds include the lost First World War training trenches on Salisbury Plain, and Britain's first double henge - discovered just down the road from Stonehenge(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Bafta-winning actress Julie Walters, of Educating Rita, Mamma Mia! and Harry Potter fame, traces her roots back to Co Mayo in western Ireland, where her great-grandfather Anthony Clark was one of the first members of the Land League. The organisation was formed in 1879 and campaigned for more than 40 years for the rights of tenant farmers to own their own land(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Nick Davies and David Harper are on hand in Reading, Berkshire to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Presented by Paul Martin(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Barbecue chefs Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel around Wales to create huge outdoor feasts to reward the work of a variety of organisations and individuals. They begin by celebrating football club Barry Town United's promotion to the Welsh Premier League, including cooking a whole pig, Hawaiian-style, in a huge brick fire pit, to be served with pit beans, cornbread and an apple and fennel slaw, with fruit-filled toffee apples for dessert(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea uses his expertise to make a dilapidated and unsafe go-kart roadworthy once again. Kirsten Ramsay faces an unusual challenge in the shape of a papier mache cat, while saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher is called upon to restore a hand-crafted leather gun slip, commissioned 30 years ago by a man as a gift for his father's 60th birthday(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as cricket enthusiasts the Wickets take on singers the Choristers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Alice Band, Whistle Whistle, Hall Book and Blass Wine(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The ninth first-round match sees four students from LSE pit their wits against a team from the Courtauld Institute of Art in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Melinda Messenger presents highlights of 1997, the year the Teletubbies hit the small screen in a blaze of colour, Bridget Jones's Diary was every woman's best friend, and Viagra gave hope to flagging men worldwide. Movies that stole the show were blockbuster Titanic and The Full Monty, which boosted both the British film industry and Sheffield's tourism(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The concluding film explores the growing tension between China and the West over allegations that the Chinese state has engaged for years in large-scale industrial espionage and hacking to steal the West's business secrets and technological know-how. China denies any such wrongdoing - but growing anger in the US is fuelling the trade war with President Xi Jinping's superpower(n)

programme.title: Golf: Solheim Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of day two of the women's team competition between Europe and USA, held this year at Gleneagles in Perthshire. During the last contest in 2017 at Des Moines Golf and Country Club, Europe began day two trailing by three points and got off to a nightmare start by losing the opening two foursomes matches. They rallied to level the session before the USA triumphed in three of the afternoon's four-ball ties to take a commanding overall five-point lead into the third day(n)

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: Wildlife series following animals that are able to defy gravity in various ways. The first programme features caracal cats, which jump to catch birds out of the air, and reveals how kangaroos' hopping movements help them find water in the desert. Leaping snakes are able to glide without wings in the jungles of Borneo while insects are able to accelerate up to 700G(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Jan and Louise find a dress belonging to Natasha, the first victim, which Anders Kjeldsen had hidden. That same night, Anders tries to kidnap another woman. Stine Velin attends her big brother Mikkel's 40th birthday party and reveals a dark family secret. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The public appeal for information on Anders Kjeldsen leads to the identification of the third victim from the farm, and an explanation of how all the women met their killer(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Following a chance encounter with a young history student, friends and metal-detecting enthusiasts Lance and Andy embark on a journey towards the discovery of a lifetime. All they need to do is get permission from a local landowner, who is rumoured to have murdered his wife. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones, with Rachael Stirling and Aimee-Ffion Edwards(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Lance and Andy keep it a secret that they are hot on the trail of the final resting place of King Sexred of the East Saxons - the Holy Grail of metal detecting. However, members of a rival club seem to already know about it, leading the gang to believe they have a mole within their ranks. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Club president Terry wants to help Andy and Lance search a paddock at Bishop's Farm, only because he is convinced that is where Larry Bishop has buried his missing wife. Meanwhile, Lance tries to get his ex-partner to see him perform at a local pub's folk music night. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: 1985 sees the 'Big Four' of 80's Pop - Spandau, Wham, Culture Club and the Durans - have a relatively quiet year which ushers in a new generation of pop talent. The show becomes a barometer for the state of the nation as the ambitious King and A-ha, propelled by the most expensive video of the year for Take On Me, compete for attention with passionate politicos Billy Bragg and The Style Council who, fired up by the ongoing miners' strike and continuing threat of nuclear war, give the studio audience their all with Between The Wars and Walls Come Tumbling Down. 1985 also sees electro dance hit the mainstream as Dead or Alive top the charts with You Spin Me Round, the first number one for producers Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and Paul Hardcastle holds the top slot for five weeks with 19, a spoken word single made with new sampling technology and released on the 10th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam war. Meanwhile, movie tie-ins and record companies eyeing a 'housewives' market see a(n)

programme.title: Glam Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the outburst of spangly pop and fuzz-filled rock that lit up the British charts in the early 1970s. Top of the Pops provided the genre's primary TV arena, adding some gloriously gaudy visual effects to the singers' already outrageous appearances. T. Rex, David Bowie, Roxy Music, Alice Cooper, Suzi Quatro, Slade, the Sweet, Elton John, Queen and Sparks are among those featured in this compilation, which also sources footage from a selection of BBC concerts as well as shows including Crackerjack and Cilla(n)

programme.title: Truth Tellers at the BBC
programme.desc: Clips from shows including The Old Grey Whistle Test, Top of the Pops, Later with Jools Holland and Britpop Now allow a nostalgic look back at some of the most outspoken, thought-provoking and lyrically gifted songsters to have visited the BBC's studios. Contributors range from socially discerning troubadours like Bob Dylan and Joni Mitchell, to modern-day poets Patti Smith and Gil Scott Heron, and rap disrupters like KRS-One, as well as more recent social observers such as Jarvis Cocker, Nick Cave and George the Poet(n)

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: Wildlife series following animals that are able to defy gravity in various ways. The first programme features caracal cats, which jump to catch birds out of the air, and reveals how kangaroos' hopping movements help them find water in the desert. Leaping snakes are able to glide without wings in the jungles of Borneo while insects are able to accelerate up to 700G(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Race and Pace: The West Indians in East Lancashire
programme.desc: Viv Richards, Wes Hall and David Lloyd recall the impact West Indian players made on the Lancashire Cricket League over the past 90 years. Don Warrington narrates(n)

programme.title: Pet Shop Boys: Live in Hyde Park
programme.desc: Neil Tennant and Chris Lowe's headline performance at Radio 2 Live In Hyde Park 2019 - the duo's only UK gig of the year, performed in front of more than 40,000 festivalgoers(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Pet Shop Boys
programme.desc: A compilation of Pet Shop Boys' performances on the pop chart programme(n)

programme.title: Cyprus Avenue
programme.desc: Eric Miller, a Belfast loyalist, mistakes his five-week-old granddaughter for Gerry Adams. Stephen Rea stars in David Ireland's award-winning dark comedy play about sectarian hatred in Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Engineering Giants
programme.desc: Tom Wrigglesworth and Rob Bell visit a dry dock in Newcastle, where they witness long-serving North Sea ferry the Pride of Bruges receiving a complete overhaul to extend its working life. The duo explore the inner workings of the 32,000-ton ship, which has transported passengers between Hull and the Belgian port of Zeebrugge for more than a quarter of a century, and travel to a ship-breaking yard to learn how ferries are recycled when they are no longer fit for service(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Tomb on Earth: Secrets of Ancient China
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Alice Roberts and Albert Lin explore the final resting place of China's first emperor, a site 200 times larger than Egypt's Valley of the Kings and home to the famous Terracotta Warriors. Making use of both the latest forensic technology and ancient texts, they delve into this historic site's secrets and uncover evidence that casts a new light on the founding of the nation(n)

programme.title: The Age of Excess: When Britain Went Too Far
programme.desc: Matthew Sweet looks at the libertinism that was rife in 18th-century London in the form of prostitutes, erotic entrepreneurs and a wide range of sex clubs. The culture was also characterised by risque ballads and the circulation of licentious pamphlets and lascivious prints(n)

programme.title: Everybody in the Place: An Incomplete History of Britain 1984-1992
programme.desc: Jeremy Deller contends that the explosion of acid house and rave in the UK was a reaction to a much wider and deeper set of fault lines in British culture. Here, the Turner Prize-winning artist upturns popular notions of the musical genres as rare and unseen archive materials map the journey from protest movements to abandoned warehouse raves, the white heat of industry bleeding into the chaotic release of the dancefloor(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues to trace the footsteps of 19th-century artist and geologist George Victor Du Noyer, examining places of power as seen through his eyes. She traces the movement of power from Newgrange, Co Meath, through the Rock of Cashel, sees Waterford Charter Roll and ends up at Castletown House in Co Kildare(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts Rome's rise from the abandonment and neglect of the 14th century into the everlasting seat of the Papacy recognised today. His story begins with the debauchery and decadence of the Renaissance, and takes viewers through the Catholic Reformation and on to the arrival of fascism and the creation of the Vatican State. He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel leads him on railway journey through the modern south Indian states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. He travels from the former princely state of Mysuru, aka Mysore, to the first stronghold of the East India Company in Chennai, formerly Madras(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: David Olusoga examines 50 years of BBC documentary archives to try and discover why dictators can have such a powerful appeal. He uncovers the surprising optimism felt by the West towards men like Gaddafi and Mugabe early in their regimes, and examines events that turned this optimism into horror(n)

programme.title: Summertime (2015)
programme.desc: An ambitious farmer's daughter travels to Paris in 1971, where she falls in love with a radical feminist. She finds herself obliged to return to the family farm, and her new lover accompanies her on the journey, but their relationship is greeted with disapproval. Romantic drama, starring Cecile De France and Izia Higelin. In French(n)

programme.desc: A compilation of songs from the series, featuring the Spartan High School Musical, the Viking Rock Band, the rapping of Charles II and Blackbeard the Pirate(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In Pembrokeshire, Kate meets the last master thatcher based in Wales. She also helps with the harvesting of water reeds - an invasive, thick grass-like plant now used on 80 per cent of thatched roofs in the UK. In Pendine Sands, Kate joins a cross-country hunt adapted for modern times and struggles to stay on the horse as they chase hounds who have been specifically trained to only pick up on human scent. Finally, Kate and Teg visit an agricultural college that helps neuro-diverse students thrive through a deep connection with the land(n)

Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Rugby World Cup
programme.desc: The former Wales forward previews the tournament in Japan and looks back at memorable moments from past tournaments. Featuring interviews with past and present players including Ken Owens, Shane Williams and Mike Phillips, plus an insight into life with Warren Gatland from his daughter Gabby(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the most important day of the year for Kerry - the annual steam fair. This year it falls on her birthday and Kurtan has organised her a surprise present. However, the gift involves some walking, and so the cousins embark on a cross-country trek. This affords them the time to share some local village myths with the crew, such as the legend of the 'Fox Twins'(n)

programme.title: Murder in Successville
programme.desc: Sleet and sidekick Emma Bunton go to North Successville, where the detective investigates the murder of a fellow cop and takes a liking to the area's chief of police. Three likely suspects soon emerge - the owner of a diner, a local fisherman and a disgraced former police officer. An approaching storm forces Sleet and Emma to seek shelter in a forest - which leads to a showdown with the killer(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, treating the audience to her own brand of humour, before introducing routines by camp comic Joe Lycett and award-winning funnyman Russell Kane(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Niklas Ekstedt, and special guest Dev Griffin. Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads to the hustle and bustle of Marrakesh to explore every side of Moroccan cuisine. She rustles up some quick dishes inspired by the aromatic flavours and smells of the medina quarter, from the mounds of olives and heady spices at the markets to the slow-cooked tagines prepared in the heart of a family kitchen and the high-end delights of Moha Fedal. one of the country's greatest chefs. Mary brings the fragrant flavours home, cooking a speedy lamb tagine, a spicy carrot hummus and a fresh summer salad(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Norwich City v Manchester City and Liverpool v Newcastle United in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents as the hunt for bargains heads to Oswestry, Shropshire, where experts Jonathan Pratt and John Cameron assist two teams of friends competing to buy three items in the hope of making the most profit at auction. Natasha also heads to Hawarden, Flintstone, to visit Gladstone's Library, Britain's only prime ministerial library, where she reveals what a wheelbarrow and a former prime minister have in common(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired detective and his wife to look for a home in Gloucestershire on a £500,000 budget, and goes behind the scenes ahead of the Badminton Horse Trials(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items bound for the skip in Walsall, featuring a Lloyd Loom-style laundry basket, a reclining chair and an oak writing bureau(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson and Leanne Crichton present the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A special programme focusing on Strictly Come Dancing's professional dancers, looking back at their journeys to reach the ballroom and some of their best moments from the show. The programme also offers viewers a behind-the-scenes look at life on Strictly, revealing some of the show's most guarded secrets(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A Radio 2 edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Jo Whiley, Gary Davies, Ken Bruce, Angela Scanlon, Craig Charles, Trevor Nelson, the Rev Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad, and a special performance from Rick Astley. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: The classical music celebration continues live from the Royal Albert Hall. Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Chorus and the BBC Singers through an energetic programme, including the Overture to Offenbach's Orpheus in the Underworld, Grainger's Marching Song of Democracy, Harold Arlen's Somewhere Over the Rainbow and Gershwin's I Got Rhythm. The event is then brought to its familiar and rousing close with Rule, Britannia!, Land of Hope and Glory and Jerusalem. Presented by Katie Derham(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v Manchester City at Carrow Road and Liverpool v Newcastle United at Anfield. The Canaries had not beaten their opponents at home since way back in August 2001 in the old First Division, although the two most recent Premier League encounters between the sides on this ground finished in very creditable 0-0 draws. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Burnley at the Amex Stadium, Manchester United v Leicester City at Old Trafford, Sheffield United v Southampton at Bramall Lane, Tottenham Hotspur v Crystal Palace at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Chelsea at Molineux(n)

programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan give the young artists the opportunity of a lifetime, as they work with Jae 5 and perform live for Lady Leshurr(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Carolina Panthers v Tampa Bay Buccaneers at Bank of America Stadium(n)

programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the sale and development of a third-floor flat in Southwark, London, a piece of land in Derbyshire's Chesterfield, and flats in Hengoed, Mid Glamorgan(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm as the hunt heads to Lincoln, with Charlie Ross and Richard Madley helping the teams, as Eric learns about one of Lincoln's most famous sons(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits the sacred Christian site of Lourdes in France, and meets a British man who believes he was miraculously cured there. Plus, a performance of Ave Maria(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a green-fingered couple in Gloucestershire find their perfect retreat with plenty of space to enjoy their hobbies, on a budget of £800,000(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich Brothers compete to design a £5,000 family garden in Southampton, ensuring it is safe for toddler Alex(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items salvaged from the country's tips and turns them into new pieces to make a profit, aided by three of the nation's master craftsmen(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series' biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOS's most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kerry Howard, Neil Stuke, Robert Duncan, Susannah Doyle, Brigit Forsyth, James Baxter, Simon Greenall and Pauline McLynn(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets a larger-than-life character in Dorset - The Cerne Abbas Giant. It's 100 years since the Giant was gifted to the National Trust, and he needs to be smartened up, so Ellie joins a group of volunteers to re-chalk the figure, hoping to restore it to its former glory. Steve Brown visits the world's only managed flock of Mute swans at Abbotsbury Swannery, while Adam Henson meets the Welsh and Scottish contenders hoping to lift this year's One Man and His Dog trophy(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where surprises include a mysterious object linked to the Titanic, a well-used watch with connections to early moon exploration and an undiscovered letter written by Winston Churchill. There are also items of museum quality, such as an exquisite piece of Indian textile and a prized collection of Gurkha medals(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of the party, Tommy reveals his intentions for Oswald Mosley, and someone close to the family finds themselves targeted. Arthur and his men arrive at Limehouse for the arranged rendezvous, but the situation has changed drastically, while Tommy visits an old war comrade who has a very specific skill that Tommy intends to put to good use. Starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Bournemouth v Everton at the Vitality Stadium and Watford v Arsenal at Vicarage Road. Bournemouth and the Toffees drew last season's corresponding fixture 2-2 after a thrilling second half that saw a red card for each team and four goals. Arsenal were looking for a fourth successive victory over the Hornets, though Watford were hindered by Troy Deeney's early sending off when they lost 1-0 at home to the Gunners last term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland is joined by Michael Stewart and Steven Thompson to present action from the weekend's Premiership fixtures, including Hamilton Academical v Celtic at Hope CBD Stadium, Rangers v Livingston at Ibrox and Aberdeen v St Johnstone at Pittodrie(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Lancashire Police's new academy, where the forensic scientists of tomorrow will train alongside the police teams solving real crimes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers pursue those responsible for an horrific crash that scarred an entire community, and raid the luxury home of an illegal money lender preying on the vulnerable(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughterare lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Phil and Theresa cling on to their last bit of hope as the court decides on baby Josh's fate. Al finds an unexpected confidante and Valerie obsesses about Daniel and Becky(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of the south east of England. Along the way, they encounter items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Landing at Glenuig, Paul Murton makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a 'dabbing coat' before landing off the Green Isle, where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart Dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Lancashire Police's new academy, where the forensic scientists of tomorrow will train alongside the police teams solving real crimes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers pursue those responsible for an horrific crash that scarred an entire community, and raid the luxury home of an illegal money lender preying on the vulnerable(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughterare lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Phil and Theresa cling on to their last bit of hope as the court decides on baby Josh's fate. Al finds an unexpected confidante and Valerie obsesses about Daniel and Becky(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of the south east of England. Along the way, they encounter items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Jo Scott helps out with this year's cauliflower harvest, while reporter Ruth Sanderson meets a group of lawnmower racing enthusiasts(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked by the hostility of the other mums at Ollie's new school, and shares her concerns with Mick. Louise hatches a plan to help Bex take her mind off university. Kathy learns Bobby is staying with Max and pleads with her grandson to reconsider. Phil and Sharon realise Mel has been taking money from the E20 account and cut her off - leaving her unable to pay for Hunter's funeral(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Rickie Haywood-Williams, legendary Motown singer Martha Reeves, Olympic gold medal-winning long jumper Greg Rutherford, actress Elizabeth Bourgine and heavyweight boxer Dillian Whyte take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. The first challenge for the five hopefuls is an Invention Test - the infamous test of imagination and creativity. The contestants are all given a mincer and a burger press and are tasked with making just one dish - a burger and fries - of their own design. Ingredients to choose from include tuna, mackerel, beef chuck, pork belly, lamb shoulder and turkey breast(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Revealing documentary following a Belfast strippogram agency and its performers - including the most popular act, Balaclava Woman(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the most important day of the year for Kerry - the annual steam fair. This year it falls on her birthday and Kurtan has organised her a surprise present. However, the gift involves some walking, and so the cousins embark on a cross-country trek. This affords them the time to share some local village myths with the crew, such as the legend of the 'Fox Twins'(n)

programme.title: Murder in Successville
programme.desc: Sleet and sidekick Emma Bunton go to North Successville, where the detective investigates the murder of a fellow cop and takes a liking to the area's chief of police. Three likely suspects soon emerge - the owner of a diner, a local fisherman and a disgraced former police officer. An approaching storm forces Sleet and Emma to seek shelter in a forest - which leads to a showdown with the killer(n)

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Proceedings in the Scottish Parliament, including a short debate to mark the 100th anniversary of the Royal Air Force Benevolent Fund, Portfolio Questions, statement on the Royal Hospital for Children and Young People and a Scottish Government-led debate on the Citizens' Assembly of Scotland, from Wednesday 11 September(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge(n)

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: A few technical glitches get sorted out as the team draws on local resources and knowledge before the distillery opens for business. The first festive season sees the community centred on the distillery peat fire, and history is made when the first Harris whisky spirit is distilled(n)
